Definition: First day of the aggregate period
Unabbreviated location name; typically the city where the test are taken
Region name where tests are taken. This is the first-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are states, provinces, or territories, etc
Place type of the returned result; most often "locality" which is an incorporated city or town political entity
Mobile Operator or ISP NameAll providers combined
Aggregation time period. Options include: Day, Week, Month, Quarter, Year
Technology platform of test taken as indicated in Speedtest Portal
The number of raw tests in the aggregate period
The number of samples included in the aggregate period
The number of unique devices included in the aggregate period.
Mean download speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
Mean upload speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
Median latency speed achieved during the time period in question (ms)
Median download speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
Median upload speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
Median latency speed achieved during the time period in question (ms)
(Legacy) The Speedscore achieved during the time period in question (Kbps).
The two-letter ISO country code for tests represented in the aggregate.
10th percentile download speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
90th percentile download speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
10th percentile upload speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
90th percentile upload speed achieved during the time period in question (Kbps)
10th percentile latency speed achieved during the time period in question (ms)
90th percentile latency speed achieved during the time period in question (ms)
Mean jitter (variance in latency) during the time period in question (ms).
Median jitter (variance in latency) during the time period in question (ms).

The Consistency Percentage achieved during the time period in question. Consistency Percentage measures what percentage of a provider's samples equals or exceeds both a download and upload threshold. Current thresholds: Mobile 5G and Fixed: 25 Mbps download and 3 Mbps upload; Mobile (except 5G): 5 Mbps download and 1 Mbps upload.

The subregion name where the tests are taken. This is the second-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are county-level or provincial-level names. Not all countries display this information.
The result of multi-server latency for the aggregated time period, based on server selection, measured in milliseconds.
The result of multi-server jitter for the aggregated time period, based on server selection, measured in milliseconds.

The subsubregion name where the tests are taken. This is the third-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are county-level or provincial-level names. Not all countries display this information.

The ID number assigned to the place type. This field will be helpful if multiple geographies have the same name.1 = city/locality2 = neighborhood3 = sublocality level 18 = administrative area level 19 = administrative area level 210 = administrative area level 311 = administrative area level 412 = administrative area level 514 = country
The country of the ISP. Field only available in Fixed Performance Aggregate.
The country of the Carrier. Field only available in Mobile Performance Aggregate.
Unabbreviated location name; typically the city where the test is taken.
